jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of product or program management, product marketing, business development or technology experience
- Experience with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a product
- Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition
- Experience with end to end product delivery
- Experience as a product manager or owner
- Experience owning technology products
jobs.description:
Amazon.com, Inc. seeks to be Earth's most customer-centric company, where customers can find and discover anything they might want to buy online, and endeavors to offer its customers the widest of selection at low price that drives Amazon’s flywheel.

The vision of Retail Business Services (RBS) Organization at Amazon is to accelerate Amazon’s flywheel by improving customer experience and enabling our Selling Partners (SPs) to grow their business with Amazon. RBS provides catalog augmentation and correction technologies for the Amazon selling community. Our solutions ensure information in Amazon's catalogs is both complete and comprehensive enough to give our customers a great shopping experience every time.

RBS is looking for a Senior Product Manager to lead the Product Strategy for RBS Growth Products. These products enable teams to
find Shopping Mission defects that lead to Customer drop off. The Senior Product Manager is a bar-raising communicator, both verbal and written, and is a well-respected leader within the business. This role requires the ability to influence across stakeholder teams to drive alignment and requires a highly effective and established leader to navigate effectively. This role requires clear articulation of complex problems and proposed solutions and requires high judgement to ensure the right leaders are involved in decisions and regularly informed on progress. This role aligns teams towards clear priorities and advocates for investment delivering large, complex business initiatives, with clear mechanisms that audit progress towards shared goals and regularly reports results to ensure long-term business success. The lead will build and scale products to uncover critical defects across the Amazon shopping journey: spanning across product discovery, evaluation, and purchase completion. These systemic problems impact the customer shopping journey- from how customers discover and evaluate products to their ability to complete purchases. This role will be at the center of collaboration between multiple world wide teams in re-imagining the experience for customers.

Key requirements for this role:

-5+ years of product or program management experience

-5+ years of blending product and program management skills to execute strategic initiatives about process creation, standardization, and improvement experience

-Bachelor's degree

-Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition

-Experience with end to end product delivery

-Experience with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a product

-Experience as a product manager or owner

-Experience owning technology products

jobs.basic_qualifications:

- Experience managing technical products or online services

jobs.description:
Imagine leading strategic initiatives that revolutionize online shopping experiences for everyday items, with the potential to impact millions of customers globally. As a Product Manager, you'll be at the forefront of creating innovative solutions that make shopping seamless, convenient, and delightful.

Key job responsibilities
Drive end-to-end product management for consumer experience initiatives from conception to global launch
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop and implement innovative shopping solutions
Create and track comprehensive success metrics to measure product effectiveness
Design scalable processes and frameworks for global implementation
Build strategic relationships across business, technology, and operations teams

A day in the life
Your day will be dynamic and impactful, spanning global teams and cutting-edge projects. You'll collaborate across design, technology, and operations, solving complex challenges and driving customer-centric innovations. From design review sessions to strategic planning, every moment is an opportunity to make a meaningful difference.

About the team
We are the Everyday Essentials team - a passionate group of problem-solvers dedicated to reimagining customer shopping experiences. Our team thrives on innovation, data-driven insights, and a deep commitment to customer satisfaction. We believe in creating solutions that make daily life easier and more convenient.

jobs.description:
Looking for a Sr Product Manager for the Travel Expense Reimbursement Audit (TERA) and the Expense vertical in Finance Operations. We are building AI/ML based Amazon-scale products which will improve user experience and generate opex savings.

As a Sr Product Manager, you will be responsible for identifying opportunities to build and enhance TERA and Expense products. You are passionate about Fraud prevention and detection and controllership while keeping a high bar on user experience. You are comfortable dealing with ambiguity, able to think big (while paying careful attention to detail), and enjoy working in a fast-paced and global team. You can manage both 1P and 3P relationships with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. You will drive product discussions, decisions and artifacts (e.g. vision, tenets, roadmaps, goals, xBRs, PRFAQs, BRDs) with executive representatives. You will work with tech (e.g. SDEs, DSs, DEs, BIEs/BAs, UXDs) and non-tech (e.g. program managers) stakeholders both within Amazon and 3Ps. You understand the trade-offs between value, time & effort related to product development and features. You own socialization of products and driving adoption strategy. You own reporting of success metrics and audit mechanisms (e.g., KPIs, reporting, evaluation standards, self-inspection). You must have strong communication skills (listening and articulating). Finally, you have a high bar and insist on the highest standards. You'll have a unique opportunity to collaborate with teams across organizations, geographies, and function, helping build exceptional product and programs that help shape the strategic direction of TERA and Expense.

Key job responsibilities
• Author compelling PRFAQs for new think big plans on ideas uncovered by the cross-functional teams.
• Understand customer, operational, and program requirements to author business requirements. Drive the creation of product strategy documents and execution. Own the discussion with business and technical stakeholder and leaders.
• Develop and maintain the Fraud prevention and detection product development plan. Facilitate planning sessions.
• Creating the needed input and output metrics to support launches/proposal and productionizing while working with BIEs/BAs and DEs. Ensure timely and accurate reporting to needed stakeholders.
• Support tech teams during scoping, design and product build (e.g. driving trade-offs with cross-functional stakeholders, managing 3P conversations).
• Own product launch protocols along with cross-functional teams. (e.g. UAT, PVT, Dial-up, periodical updates etc).
• Own the product socialization and adoption strategy and execution through interactions with customers (e.g. trainings, demo sessions, onboarding material).

jobs.description:
Amazon Global Selling Experience team is seeking an experienced Product Manager to for an exciting new program focused on cross-border selling and international expansion. You will be responsible for driving the Seller Experience for Amazon Global Selling. Amazon Global Selling is the next frontier for the marketplace and allows sellers to sell across the Globe. You will be responsible for driving the product strategy, product vision, roadmap, implementation (with technology teams), product marketing and feature adoption.

You will need to develop a deep understanding of cross-border trade, regulations, logistics, payments and seller/customer needs.



Key job responsibilities
Product strategy and direction: Continually assess the needs of sellers, market trends, competitive opportunities and threats in defining and evolving the Amazon Global Selling product strategy in your area of product ownership. Define both long term strategy and short term plans to achieve the product vision and deliver business results. You will also contribute to the overall business and marketing strategy in your area of focus.
· Roadmap Planning: Ensure consistent execution of product lifecycle processes from concept to launch. Provide value-based prioritization of product development initiatives to ensure maximum ROI and market-driven alignment. Deliver world-class innovation in features, usability and product performance in consistent cadence of releases. Manage a cross-functional process for prioritizing, delivering and launching major product releases.
· Requirements Definition: Develop comprehensive product plans, incorporating market requirements, define the competitive arena, and establish the objectives, strategies, and actions required to achieve business goals. Produce high quality requirements documents and effectively prioritize and manage the scope of each product launch to deliver key capabilities on time and on budget. Writes user stories to communicate customer needs in an Agile development environment. Work closely with Legal and Tax on regulations and compliance requirements to eliminate seller adoption bottlenecks.
· Product Roll-out: Create and execute go-to-market plans for new features. Monitor key performance metrics to ensure product performance and identify optimisation opportunities. Partner closely with the business teams to drive adoption and ensure processes for on boarding that are easy and intuitive.

A day in the life
The ideal candidate will be enthusiastic about managing challenging, complex and ambiguous projects across multiple teams and locations. We are looking for a tenured Product leader who shares Amazon's passion for the customer—someone who understands the importance of compelling features and functionalities in driving great experience. In addition, the candidate will also be able to influence without authority and earn trust of a wide range of cross-functional, tech/non-tech stakeholders.

jobs.description:
Lead the transformation of enterprise decision-making at Amazon by building the Amazon Sentiment Analyzer (ASA), a premier intelligence hub for Amazon’s senior leaders across the Stores and Devices Organization. As Principal Product Manager for ASA, you'll own the product strategy and execution for an intuitive interface that provides real-time access to CX and business intelligence insights derived from over 5 billion customer, seller, and operational interactions annually. This is a high-visibility role with exposure to Amazon's senior leadership, including VPs across retail categories, operations, and product teams. You'll be responsible for bridging the gap between leadership intuition and data-driven evidence, enabling faster strategic decisions that directly impact customer experience.

Key job responsibilities
• Own the end-to-end product strategy for ASA, from current pilot phase through enterprise-wide deployment across Amazon's leadership ecosystem
• Define and execute the product roadmap that transforms ASA into an essential daily workflow tool for senior leaders
• Drive the integration strategy with relevant tools, systems and processes to embed ASA into critical decision-making forums
• Partner with senior leadership to identify high-impact use cases and measure success through reduced decision-making time and increased insight exploration
• Design and execute a comprehensive GTM strategy; Define success metrics and KPIs that demonstrate ASA's impact on decision-making speed and quality
• Drive user research and feedback collection from senior leaders to continuously improve the conversational AI interface and insight delivery

jobs.description:
Selling Partner Services continues to be high-growth and strategically important business within WW Stores org. Seller Fees team is responsible for developing science and tech products that enable this $100B+ WW business, and raise the bar on our Selling Partners experience.

Within Seller Fees, this Sr. PMT will own key products that improve the integrity of fees charged to Sellers by envisioning new products and leveraging GenAI. This PMT will partner closely with engineering, science and UX teams to develop best-in-class products for Amazon to charge fees to Sellers. This role will support WW FBA teams across 20+ stores and be the voice of seller to improve their experience of selling on Amazon.

Key job responsibilities
1. Independently envision and execute large projects from start to finish including customer problem discovery, data gathering and analysis, designing creative solutions, defining product requirements and managing technical execution.
2. Engage with science, engineering, and business stakeholders and customers throughout the entire product lifecycle.
3. Manage prioritization and trade-offs among competing high value opportunities.
4. Contribute to roadmap and OP1/OP2 planning.
5. Own leadership review documents including PRFAQs, vision documents, monthly or quarterly business reviews.

A day in the life
Each day can look different depending on the product lifecycle, but here's an example:
1. Review PRFAQ with senior leaders
2. Define product requirements for a new feature
3. Dive deep into a seller escalation with regards to existing functionality
4. Provide updates for a QBR/MBR
5. Analyze data to quantify impact from a recently launched feature

jobs.description:
As product manager for quick commerce supply chain, you will build solutions to power a nimble and agile supply chain that ships millions of units every day. Amazon Now (quick commerce) is well funded startup in Amazon, where we are challenging the norms to deliver on critical mission to make quick commerce work for Amazon.

You will work on interesting problem statements across hub operations. You are expected to work through ambiguity, cut through clutter to find right problems to solve and partner with stakeholders across operations, tech and business (instock, operations excellence, S&OP) to build world class solutions. All this while building with right speed.

Key job responsibilities
1. Evaluate supply chain problem space in context of business needs as per evolution of business to find right opportunities in hub operations space.
2. Experiment solutions that maximize hub productivity to deliver right instock
3. Create and deliver a robust roadmap to deliver right optimization solutions by being proficient in operations research

jobs.title: Product Manager , Amazon Now

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of product management experience
- Experience managing technical products or online services
- Experience in product, program, or project management in leading cross-functional teams in delivery of major new products or services
jobs.description:
Are you passionate about innovating on behalf of Customers? Does working with latest AI and translation technologies excite you? Do you want to have a direct impact on how customers shop on Amazon across world-wide stores? Come join us!

ASIN Localization team is seeking an experienced Product Manager to help define best-in-class localization experience for Amazon Customers and Selling Partners globally. You will define business and high-level technical requirements, execute projects, own prioritization, and also understand the impact of your proposals on customer experience and wider Amazon business. You will work closely with engineering, science and business leaders to set strategy, define priorities, and make the hard, high-judgment trade-offs between features and time-to-market.

This is an exciting opportunity to join a fast-paced team, and is truly a cross-functional role where you’ll have significant responsibility and the ability to drive Amazon’s worldwide growth via technology.

Key job responsibilities
The ideal candidate will be passionate about improving Customer Shopping experience, with a track record of success in delivering products and services. They will have thrived and succeeded in delivering broadly adopted technology products/services in a very high growth environment where priorities shift quickly. They will use A/B testing when needed, have a solid judgement of one-way vs. two-way door decisions and will have an affinity towards iterating fast and releasing often. We are looking for a product manager who can prioritize well, communicate clearly and compellingly and who understands how to drive a high level of focus and excellence with a strong, talented, opinionated team. This product manager will have a strong track record of balancing innovative thinking with a strong customer and quality focus.

A day in the life
You will start some days diving deep into customer and selling partner feedback, analyzing data to uncover valuable insights that drive product decisions. Your morning might include a strategic planning session where you define product vision. By midday, you're leading a cross-functional team meeting, orchestrating inputs from engineers, applied scientists, and operations teams to evolve your product roadmap. After lunch, you're reviewing performance metrics of recently launched features, making data-driven decisions. The afternoon might see you whiteboarding scalable technical solutions with your engineering team, ensuring global applicability. You'll spend time validating quick experiments, embracing the fail-fast mentality to learn and iterate rapidly. Regular check-ins with stakeholders keep everyone aligned on progress and priorities. As the day wraps up, you're already planning for tomorrow's review of your product proposal (PR-FAQ) with Product and Finance leadership, always keeping customer experience at the forefront. It's a dynamic role where you'll constantly balance strategic thinking with tactical execution, all while maintaining a relentless focus on delivering value at scale.

About the team
The ASIN Localization team is responsible for translating and localizing product listings into multiple languages and making them available for sale on all of our global eCommerce sites. We translate at scale using leading edge machine translation. We work with stakeholders across Amazon's world-wide stores and manage software services and systems that interact with many of Amazon’s core technologies, including detail page, catalog, pricing, fulfillment, compliance, selling partner tools, and customer service. If you’ve bought something on Amazon in the past, you have probably seen some of our work.
- Direct impact on the Customer shopping experience
- World-wide remit
- Dedicated engineering team

jobs.description:
Sr. Product Manager, IN Prime Acquisitions

How often have you had an opportunity to build the acquisition growth engine for Prime - one of the largest and loved subscription programs in India? We are looking for someone who is passionate and obsessed about customers, understands their needs and is looking for opportunities to build world class products that will help customers join Prime and get the best of benefits across shopping, shipping, entertainment, music and more. If you are someone who thrives in ambiguity, and balances dive deep with high judgement, looking around the corners to invent around constraints - we have the role for you. The Senior Product Manager for India Prime Acquisitions will manage and define the end to end roadmap of who, how and when we upsell Prime to customers on the Amazon app. Not only will the role play a defining role in devising the growth strategy for Prime in India, the role is expected to influence other worldwide locales with worldwide first innovations.

In this context, India Prime is looking for an experienced Product leader for Prime member Acquisitions. You will lead the product vision ,strategy and own the product portfolio for this business in India. You will deliver a multi-year product charter for renewals across different plans and channels. Role offers an exciting intellectual opportunity to solve for multiple whitespaces and steer your career pace as we scale.

Responsibilities:
- Responsible for defining a customer insight , data driven product vision, roadmap , write BRDs, user stories , technical design reviews, prioritizing and executing in a high expectation environment.
- Work with technology (IN and global) to build exceptional products. Anticipating bottlenecks, independently drive tradeoffs decisions balancing business needs versus technical or operational constraints.
- Collaborate/influence various stakeholders at multiple levels , local/global , functions- tech, UX, Marketing , Legal , Finance , Machine Learnings, Data sciences, Amazon wide business teams.
- Own reporting to leadership through launch announcements, monthly updates, status updates.
- Develop the long-term strategy for your portfolio, aligning it to the goals of the organization and translate them into an achievable multi – year roadmap.
